component.name: BigLinux Microphone
component.summary: AI-powered microphone noise reduction for PipeWire

description.p: BigLinux Microphone inserts a PipeWire filter chain on the default audio source, powered by the GTCRN deep-learning speech enhancement plugin. The result is a clean, noise-free microphone signal for every application — meetings, streams, voice notes — with no need to pick a virtual source manually.

ul.li: GTCRN neural-network noise reduction with DNS3 and VCTK models.
Silence gate and broadcast-style compressor tuned for voice.
WebRTC acoustic echo cancellation (opt-in) for calls without headphones.
Output filter that cleans every sound the system plays before it reaches your speakers — useful for noisy meeting calls without changing the default audio device.
Live spectrum analyser driven by the PipeWire graph.
Zero manual routing — relies on WirePlumber 0.5 smart filters.
Plasma 6 system-tray applet for one-click toggling.

description.p: Full Rust rewrite. Drops the legacy Python stack in favour of WirePlumber smart-filter integration, a typed settings model, conditional GTCRN/pitch/gate nodes (loaded only when the user actually enables them), VAD-aware noise-reduction strength, equalizer presets, voice changer with preset marks, real-time spectrum analyser, opt-in WebRTC echo cancellation, and a background service with proper systemd hardening.
